will be protected. Freight traffic
would not be tied UP, were 1t not
for the fact that the switchmen's
union have the contract on the
yards and an agreement exists be-
tween this union and the Brother
hood of Railroad Trainraen by
which the latter order is prevented
from making up trains or aiding in
any way with the switching. The
force of officials and yard foremen
Is insufficient to do that work.


Word has not yet been received
from the Canadian Pacific officials
and 1t is not yet certain that the
switchmen on this road win remain
at work, Neither the B. OF r. 7.
nor the switchmen's union has con-
tracts with the Canadian Pacific, It
is stated. The o. r. & N. is oper-
ating as usual, both the road and
yard contracts all along this line
being held by the B. Of r. T.


A dispatch was received by MEM
bers of the switchmen's union here
last night, stating that practically
every one of the IL big roads rum
ming into the west will be affected
before the trouble is over.


Spokane and the Inland Empire
must suffer greatly II the trouble
continues long, for there is reported
to be but about two weeks supply
Of fuel within reach Of the consum-
ers and many lines Of groceries will
be depleted before that time. Much
OF the city's Christmas goods are.
on their way to Spokane.
